messages.content: Hey — I just switched jobs recently and would like your help getting my communications right.

Congrats on the new role — nice move. I can help with that. What specific communications do you want help with (e.g., LinkedIn announcement, intro message to your new team, first-one‑on‑one with your manager, status updates, email templates, Slack tone)? Also tell me the audience and preferred tone (formal, friendly, concise), or paste a draft you want revised.
I want to prepare two short pieces: a private note to my previous team and a public announcement to my professional contacts.

Great — I can do that. A few quick questions so the drafts match what you want:

- Do you want to name your previous team/company and your new company/title in either message?
- Tone preferences: private note — warm/personal or formal/concise? public announcement — celebratory, strictly professional, or brief and factual?
- Private note: will it be sent individually to people or as a group message? Any specific colleagues or achievements you want to call out (or anything to avoid)?
- Where will the public announcement appear (LinkedIn post, email to contacts, or something else)?
- Length: one short paragraph each, or up to two?

Tell me the answers or paste any details you want included, and I’ll draft both.
For both, I'm aiming for a warm, professional tone — appreciative and concise.

Private note to your previous team (group-friendly, can be sent individually with minor edits)

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new role at [New Company] and will be moving on from [Previous Company]. Thank you for the support, collaboration, and everything we accomplished together — I’m proud of our work and will miss being part of this team. Wishing you all continued success; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).

Public announcement to professional contacts (LinkedIn-friendly)

I’m excited to share that I’ve joined [New Company] as [Title]. I’m grateful to my colleagues at [Previous Company] for their support and the opportunities that helped me get here — thank you. Looking forward to this next chapter and to connecting with anyone interested in [area/industry/goal].
The private note should mention I'm available to help with handover and say thanks for the collaboration.

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new role at [New Company] and will be moving on from [Previous Company]. Thank you for the collaboration and support — I’m proud of what we accomplished together. I’m available to help with the handover, so please reach out if there’s anything I can do before I leave. Wishing you all continued success; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).
The public message should be suitable for a general audience; nothing company-specific or technical.

Private note to your previous team

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new role and will be moving on from [Previous Company]. Thank you for the collaboration and support — I’m proud of what we accomplished together. I’m available to help with the handover, so please reach out if there’s anything I can do before I leave. Wishing you all continued success; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).

Public announcement for a general audience

I’m excited to share that I’ve started a new role. I’m grateful to my former colleagues for their support and the opportunities that helped me grow — thank you. Looking forward to this next chapter and to connecting with friends and colleagues.
I'd also prefer to keep personal names minimal and avoid sharing compensation or negotiation details.

Got it — I’ll keep personal names minimal and omit any compensation/negotiation details. Here are the revised drafts:

Private note to your previous team

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new role and will be moving on from [Previous Company]. Thank you for the collaboration and support — I’m proud of what we accomplished together. I’m available to help with the handover, so please reach out if there’s anything I can do before I leave. Wishing you all continued success; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).

Public announcement for a general audience

I’m excited to share that I’ve started a new role. I’m grateful to my former colleagues for their support and the opportunities that helped me grow — thank you. Looking forward to this next chapter and to connecting with friends and colleagues.
Make the wording simple enough that someone outside the industry will understand it.

Private note to your previous team

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new job and will be leaving [Previous Company]. Thank you for the teamwork and support — I’m proud of what we achieved together. I’m available to help with the handover, so please reach out if there’s anything I can do before I leave. Wishing you all the best; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).

Public announcement for a general audience

Hello — I’m happy to share that I’ve started a new job. I’m grateful to my former colleagues for their support and what I learned while working with them — thank you. I’m looking forward to this next chapter and to staying connected with friends and colleagues.
Keep the pieces short — ideally a single short paragraph each so I can paste them into an email and a post.

Private note to your previous team

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new job and will be leaving [Previous Company]. Thank you for the teamwork and support — I’m proud of what we achieved together. I’m happy to help with the handover, so please reach out if there’s anything I can do before I go. Wishing you all the best; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).

Public announcement for a general audience

Hello — I’m happy to share that I’ve started a new job. I’m grateful to my former colleagues for their support and what I learned while working with them — thank you. I’m looking forward to this next chapter and to staying connected with friends and colleagues.
I don't want them to read like a brag list; more a friendly sign-off and a positive look ahead.

Private note to your previous team

Hi everyone — I wanted to let you know I’ve accepted a new job and will be leaving [Previous Company]. Thank you for the teamwork and support — I’m grateful for what we accomplished together. I’m happy to help with the handover, so please reach out if there’s anything I can do before I go. Wishing you all the best; please keep in touch (LinkedIn: [link] / email: [email]).

Public announcement for a general audience

Hello — I wanted to share that I’ve started a new job. Thank you to my former colleagues for their support and what I learned while working with them. I’m looking forward to this next chapter and to staying connected with friends and colleagues.
If that's clear, I can ask you to draft them in a minute.
Clear — I’m ready whenever you are to draft them.
Packing up my desk felt oddly emotional — I found a scribbled to-do from years ago that made me laugh.
That’s a nice moment — small finds like that can stick with you. Glad it made you laugh.
A coworker gave me a ridiculous oversized mug on my last day; I swear it's bigger than my head.
That's hilarious — sounds like a legendary farewell mug. Perfect conversation piece; hope it gave you a good laugh on your last day.
On the way out someone taped a goofy photo montage on my monitor — totally unexpected and sweet.
That’s really sweet — little surprises like that make goodbyes feel warm. Hope it left you smiling.
I gave my small desk plant to one of the newer hires; it felt strange parting with something so tiny.
That was a nice gesture — even small things can carry a lot of meaning, and it’s perfectly normal to feel a little odd handing them off.
My commute changed quite a bit: a longer ride but I get to walk through a small park now and that helps.
That sounds like a worthwhile trade-off — a longer ride, but a peaceful walk through the park can be a great way to start or end the day and make the change feel more manageable.
HR had one last form that needed an extra signature; it took longer than I thought and I nearly missed a meeting.
Ugh — that’s frustrating. Last‑minute HR paperwork can really throw off your day; hope you were able to make the meeting or reschedule without too much hassle.
The new office sent onboarding swag — a giant hoodie and a notebook with a logo I can't quite pronounce.
Nice — that sounds like a fun welcome. Giant hoodies are the best for cozy commutes, and a logo you can’t pronounce is a perfect icebreaker.
My partner joked that I'm collecting company hoodies like merit badges; maybe they're right.
Haha — that’s a perfect line. Hoodies as merit badges — comfy, practical, and a fun way to remember each stop.
I spent an afternoon sorting old emails and deleting clutter; the archive is suddenly more organized than my desk.
Nice — that must feel satisfying. A tidier inbox can make things feel a bit lighter, even if the desk hasn’t caught up yet.
I bumped into a former teammate in the elevator and we traded little tips about recurring meetings and calendar tricks.
Those quick hallway—or elevator—catch-ups are the best; little calendar tips can save so much hassle. Glad you bumped into them.
I heard that at the new place they do board games on Fridays — not what I expected, but it sounds fun.
That sounds fun — a relaxed, low‑pressure way to meet people and unwind. Hope you get to join in a game or two.
I've been keeping a running note of onboarding questions, but sometimes I overthink how to phrase something.
That's totally normal — it's easy to overthink wording when you want to make a good impression. If you'd like, paste a few of your questions and I can help make them clear and concise.
My cat walked across the keyboard during a last-week video call and somehow stole the show.
That's hilarious — cats have perfect timing for steals-the-show moments. Bet it gave everyone a good laugh.
